input.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

payload.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

source_payload.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

floor-level tea nook — floor cushions + bamboo screens
neutral Japandi living — low sofa, sculptural wooden coffee table

pins.title: Japandi Dining Room with Neutral Decor
pins.description: A Japandi dining room with neutral décor blends Japanese simplicity and Scandinavian warmth. Soft beige, warm wood, and gentle earth tones create a calm, inviting space. Clean lines, natural materials, and minimal furnishings keep the room feeling open and uncluttered, while subtle textures like linen, ceramic, and light wood add quiet elegance and comfort.
pins.cluster_label: light-wood Japandi dining — round table + woven pendant

pins.title: Japandi nook area
pins.cluster_label: floor-level tea nook — floor cushions + bamboo screens

pins.title: Japandi Living Room: Calm Minimal Decor, Natural Wood & Indoor Plants
pins.description: Calm Japandi living room inspiration with a light neutral sofa, low wooden coffee table, soft textured rug, and plenty of indoor plants for a serene, balanced space. Minimal wall art, natural materials, and clean lines create an airy, soothing aesthetic—perfect for Japanese style interiors, minimalist living room ideas, and warm neutral home decor. Save for Japandi decor and plant-filled living room styling.
pins.cluster_label: neutral Japandi living — low sofa, sculptural wooden coffee table

cluster_summary.label: light-wood Japandi dining — round table + woven pendant
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Round oak dining table, curved mid-height upholstered chairs in linen textures, woven rattan pendant for warm upward glow; staging uses ceramic tableware, single sculptural vase and subtle wall wood slatting to elongate vertical lines.

cluster_summary.label: floor-level tea nook — floor cushions + bamboo screens
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Intimate floor seating with oversized cushioned loungers, layered natural fiber rugs and bamboo/shoji screening; low wooden tray table, ambient paper lanterns and small green plantings to create a cozy, meditative corner.

cluster_summary.label: neutral Japandi living — low sofa, sculptural wooden coffee table
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Light neutral linen sofa on low wooden base, asymmetrical sculpted oak coffee table, textured area rug and potted greenery; pared-back art and vertical wood slat accents emphasize calm proportion and organic materiality.

vibe_report.summary: A calm, warm Japandi interior that balances minimalist Japanese restraint with Scandinavian coziness. Light woods, soft warm neutrals, and tactile natural fabrics create a serene backdrop for a young family. Scenes emphasize low, grounded furniture, vertical wood slatting accents, and a small, well-integrated work corner while keeping circulation clear and uncluttered.
vibe_report.palette_rationale: A warm-neutral palette (beiges, soft ochres, muted greys and deep espresso accents) reinforces hygge warmth while keeping contrast low and organic. Accent tones are limited to natural wood grains and deep brown for scale and grounding.
vibe_report.lighting_mood: Soft ambient daylight from large exterior glazing combined with a few warm practicals (woven pendant over dining, paper lanterns or floor lamp in nook). Directional daylight should sculpt volumes; practical fixtures provide warm fill in evening shots.
materials: light oak and ash wood (flooring, low furniture)
linen and cotton textiles (sofas, cushions, curtains)
natural stone or honed concrete (kitchen counters accent)
textures: light wood grain with soft lacquer
furniture_language: low-profile sofa and coffee table with sculptural organic forms
round dining table with curved back chairs
slim, vertical bookshelves and minimalist storage
compact standing desk with simple wooden top
styling_rules: Limit decorative objects to single sculptures, one ceramic vase, and one small plant per vignette.
Keep wall art minimal — one vertical piece or a narrow wood slat panel to emphasize height.
Place crib in Dream bedroom near but not blocking the window to allow daylight and maintain clear circulation.
Work corner must feel integrated: standing desk against a side wall with a slim bookshelf and soft task lighting.
Use layered rugs to define floor-level nook or play area without blocking pathways.
avoid: high-gloss finishes or bright saturated colors
overly busy patterns or dense cluttered shelving
floating furniture that blocks natural circulation
introducing theatrical or impossible light sources

room_guidance.headline: Grounded family living — low, breathable, and flexible
room_guidance.guidance: Stage a low neutral linen sofa facing the glass opening to the Relax patio, a sculptural oak coffee table centrally placed on a textured neutral rug, and a tall slim bookshelf adjacent to the work corner. The standing desk may be placed in the living-area corner closest to the hallway for a visible yet tidy work nook. Keep a small play rug near the sofa. Use one large potted plant near the patio doors to frame the view.

room_guidance.headline: Quiet restful bedroom with family-ready crib corner
room_guidance.guidance: Arrange a simple low bed on the long axis, place the crib on one side near the exterior window so it benefits from morning light without blocking it. Include a soft reading lamp and a compact dresser. Keep textiles layered and warm; add a small work corner only if the standing desk cannot be accommodated in the living area.

room_guidance.headline: Light-wood Japandi dining with woven pendant focus
room_guidance.guidance: Place a round oak dining table under a woven rattan pendant. Use three to four curved linen-upholstered chairs. Keep the bay windows unobstructed and use a single sculptural vase or ceramic set as a center staging object. Let natural daylight be the primary source; pendant provides warm fill for evening shots.

room_guidance.headline: Minimal kitchen with tactile surfaces and quiet utility
room_guidance.guidance: Keep counters nearly clear—one or two ceramic canisters and a wooden cutting board. If visible from the dining bay, show material transitions: light wood cabinets, honed stone counters, and matte black fixtures. Use practical under-cabinet lighting or a single pendant over an island if present.

room_guidance.headline: Bright sunroom/patio with floor-level comfort
room_guidance.guidance: Create a tea-nook vignette with floor cushions, a low wooden tray table, and a bamboo screen or light slatted divider. Use plants to create privacy and softness. Keep the glass doors visible and clear to preserve the indoor-outdoor connection.
must_avoid: large outdoor furniture that overshadows the nook

warnings: Several room sizes and window placements are inferred from the floor plan graphic — verify physical window locations before final staging.
Small service rooms and closets have low natural light; avoid detailed color grading that implies strong daylight there.

creative_spec.overall_mood: Soft organic warmth — calm, meditative, family-ready Japandi
global_style_notes: Preserve all architectural geometry and verticals during image pass and video generation.
Derive lighting strictly from visible windows and practical fixtures; do not add new light sources that contradict the floor plan.
Favor editorial image correction pass for low-contrast interior photos to achieve crisp sculpted shadows and preserve depth.
Maintain believable furniture scale relative to measured room dimensions (e.g., Nest: ~12' x 11.7', Dream: ~12.5' x 11.6', Dine: ~7.83' x 8').
